title: Restio adpressus
taxonomy: Restio adpressus is classified as a species of sedge in the family Restionaceae. This classification is part of the larger plant kingdom, where it is placed in the division Magnoliophyta, class Liliopsida, and order Poales. The family Restionaceae is a group of monocotyledonous plants that are commonly found in southern hemisphere regions. Restio adpressus is native to South Africa, where it grows in moist, sandy areas. The specific classification of Restio adpressus reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
